purpose: Defines the !help slash command — outputs a categorized practical guide to Synapse usage. Groups commands into Context & Discovery, Parallel Execution, Auditing & Analysis, Monitoring, and Project Management sections with one-line descriptions. Then provides Tips for Best Results (start with context, parallelize when sensible, be specific in prompts, keep CLAUDE.md current, run !env_check periodically, use !toc_update after structural changes, bootstrap PKI with !learn) and Workflow Patterns (small/large feature, very-large with master_plan_track, first-time-on-project, start-of-session, code-review workflow, blast-radius analysis, periodic maintenance). Master adapts output to actual project state — omits sections for unavailable commands.
gotchas: Adapt the guide to actual project state — if certain commands don't exist (e.g., no PKI yet), omit those sections rather than reference unavailable features.
This is a STATIC reference doc — the content of the guide is defined inline in this .md file, not generated dynamically (unlike !commands).
Workflow Patterns section is high-value — it lists exact command sequences for common scenarios.
patterns: Tip+pattern format: numbered tips → workflow recipes.
